items.briefing:
Google has identified a flaw in a tool called KnowledgeDeliver and is warning of a surge in Chinese-linked phishing attacks — both relevant reminders to stay cautious with unexpected emails, especially those requesting login details or file access. Separately, Microsoft has released an urgent unscheduled patch for SharePoint; if your organisation uses SharePoint, check with your IT team to ensure it has been applied promptly.

A new report also highlights that many Australian executives still treat AI as purely an IT concern rather than a whole-of-organisation responsibility — worth raising in your next team discussion. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email asking you to click a link or enter credentials, verify the request by contacting the sender directly through a known phone number or separate email.

featured.title: Kinetic IT report flags AI & sovereign execution gap
featured.quote: Australian government agencies lack funding and skills to safely deploy AI systems securely.
